revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama]][[ja:出雲伊波比神社]][[ro:Izumo Iwai]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama | native_name = 出雲伊波比神社 | image = Izumo Iwai Shrine 09.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama | coordinates = {{coord|35.942847|139.311317}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|ca|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|es|Ōkuninushi|fa|اوکونینوشی|fr|Ōkuninushi|hu|Ókuninusi|id|Ookuninushi|it|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|ko|오오쿠니누시|mni|ꯑꯣꯀꯨꯅꯤꯅꯨꯁꯤ|pl|Ōkuninushi|pt|Ookuninushi|ru|Окунинуси|simple|Ōkuninushi|sr|Okuninuši|sv|Ōkuninushi|tr|Ōkuninushi|uk|Окунінуші|vi|Ōkuninushi|zh|大國主|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama'''|出雲伊波比神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 19 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039737|ltq=Q135039737|qid=Q135039737|1=Ihahino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039739|ltq=Q135039739|qid=Q135039739|1=Nakahikahano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Moroyama, Saitama|lt=Moroyama|ar|موروياما|arz|موروياما|ceb|Moroyama-machi|de|Moroyama (Saitama)|en|Moroyama, Saitama|es|Moroyama|fa|مورویاما، سایتاما|fr|Moroyama|id|Moroyama, Saitama|it|Moroyama|ja|毛呂山町|ko|모로야마정|ms|Moroyama, Saitama|my|မိုရိုယာမမြို့၊ ဆိုင်းတမ|pl|Moroyama|ru|Морояма|sv|Moroyama|tl|Moroyama, Saitama|tr|Moroyama|uk|Морояма (Сайтама)|zh|毛呂山町|qid=Q1354799|1=Moroyama, Saitama}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Iruma District, Saitama|lt=Iruma district|ceb|Iruma-gun|en|Iruma District, Saitama|fr|District d'Iruma|id|Distrik Iruma, Saitama|it|Distretto di Iruma|ja|入間郡|ko|이루마군|pl|Powiat Iruma|ru|Ирума (уезд)|uk|Повіт Ірума|zh|入間郡|qid=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.942847|139.311317}} == image (P18) == * Izumo Iwai Shrine 09.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q276944|ltq=Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181707 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いずもいわいじんじゃhttps://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html * イツモノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == postal code (P281) == * 350-0465https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5030005010961 == Commons category (P373) == * Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Itsumono- Shrine|lt=Itsumono- Shrine|qid=Q135039738|1=Itsumono- Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県入間郡毛呂山町岩井西5-17-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県入間市宮寺', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama}} [[JA:出雲伊波比神社]] [[RO:Izumo Iwai]] {{wikidata link|Q30933323}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|A shrine enshrined in Moroyama Town, Iruma District|Other uses}} {{Shrine |Name = Izumo Iwai Shrine |Image = [[File:Izumo Iwai Shrine 09.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Address = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|qid=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Moroyama Town|Q1354799|qid=Q1354799|1=Moroyama, Saitama}} Iwai-Nishi 5-17-1 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|56|32.7|N|139|18|40.7|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Izumo Iwai Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ameno-Hohi|Q10940685|qid=Q10940685|qid=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = 53rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main hall = Ikken-sha nagare-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = November 3 |Rituals = Yabusame of Izumo Iwai Shrine |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|label=Izumo Iwai Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Izumo Iwai Shrine''' (いずもいわいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Moroyama Town|Q1354799|qid=Q1354799|1=Moroyama, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Iruma District|Q1153801|qid=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is considered one of the candidate shrines (ronsha) for the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed as “{{ill|lt=Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)|Q104539298|qid=Q104539298|lt=Izumo Iwai Shrine|1=Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)}}.” It is the only shrine in Saitama Prefecture where {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} (mounted archery) is offered annually. === History === According to shrine tradition (the “Garyūzan Gūdenki”), in the 53rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} enshrined {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} after dedicating a halberd bestowed by the emperor. Later, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}, the Musashi {{ill|lt=Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|Q2483673|qid=Q2483673|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} enshrined {{ill|lt=Ameno-Hohi|Q10940685|qid=Q10940685|1=Ame no Hohi}}, forming the deity Izumo Iwai no Kami.[http://www.town.moroyama.saitama.jp/www/contents/1285113266909/index.html Izumo Iwai Shrine] – Moroyama Town website Records in a Daijō-kan edict indicate that in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}}) of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, the shrine received offerings ({{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|qid=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}}) as an official shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed it as the foremost of the five shrines in Iruma District. The present shrine buildings were rebuilt in the year following a fire in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|qid=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|qid=Q6412|1=1527}}). === Cultural Properties === === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Main hall (with two ridge plaques): Ikken-sha nagare-zukuri, copper-sheet roofing, built in the 8th year of Daiei (1528).[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/184606 Cultural Heritage Online] Izumo Iwai Shrine 13.jpg|Main hall Izumo Iwai Shrine 12.jpg|Hachiman Shrine Izumo Iwai Shrine 02.jpg|Kagura hall Izumo Iwai Shrine 07.jpg|Purification pavilion === Yabusame === The shrine’s yabusame is said to have originated in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|qid=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} ({{ill|lt=1063|Q19400|qid=Q19400|1=1063}}), when it was dedicated by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|qid=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}.{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.moroyama.saitama.jp/www/contents/1588308692078/files/1.pdf Yabusame of Izumo Iwai Shrine]}} – Moroyama Town pamphlet As of the 2010s, it is held twice a year, in spring and autumn. In spring, a ritual called ganteki is performed in which boys under the age of seven ride a horse and shoot a single arrow. Full-scale mounted archery (yūteki) is carried out during the main autumn festival, where boys around 15 years old ride and perform three rounds of shooting using three horses (one from each festival district). Date and time (main festival): Every year on {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}} ({{ill|lt=Culture Day|Q1009535|qid=Q1009535|1=Culture Day}}) : Morning shooting: from around 9:00 a.m. to about 10:30 a.m. : Evening shooting: from 2:30 p.m. to about 5:00 p.m. === Other === In 2016, it was reported that a large number of {{ill|lt=feral cats|Q2404562|qid=Q2404562|1=Feral cat}} had settled on the shrine grounds, causing problems.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.saitama-np.co.jp/news/2016/11/13/07.html |title=Cries at the Cat Shrine… Important Cultural Property Shrine in Moroyama, Increased Cats Scratching with Claws; Feeding Stopped (Saitama Shimbun, November 12, 2016) |accessdate=May 21, 2017 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20161113195807/http://www.saitama-np.co.jp/news/2016/11/13/07.html |archivedate=November 13, 2016 }} === Location === 〒350-0465 5-17-1 Iwai-Nishi, Moroyama Town, Iruma District, Saitama Prefecture === Access === * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Morō Station|Q844988|qid=Q844988|1=Moro Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hachikō Line|Q1372071|qid=Q1372071|1=Hachikō Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Morō Station|Q11527554|qid=Q11527554|1=Higashi-Moro Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Ogose Line|Q2782450|qid=Q2782450|1=Tobu Ogose Line}} * About 15 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tsurugashima Interchange|Q11174029|qid=Q11174029|1=Tsurugashima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|Q1059893|qid=Q1059893|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} or {{ill|lt=Ken-Ō Tsurugashima Interchange|Q11422768|qid=Q11422768|1=Ken-o-Tsurugashima interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Metropolitan Inter-City Expressway|Q5957728|qid=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} === Notes === = == See Also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama}} * [https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8953 Izumo Iwai Shrine | Shrines of Saitama Prefecture] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Izumoiwai Shrine, Moroyama]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:毛呂山町の建築物]] [[Category:毛呂山町の歴史]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]
